title: Cloud Run Revisions, Validation, Traffic, Rollback, and Recovery · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Cloud Run Revision Image Contract
summary: A pipeline deploys latest, routes all traffic before readiness and smoke checks, changes IAM with application release, cannot identify the prior digest, and calls redeployment a rollback. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci cloud run revision image contract increment in progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option.
Bound token lifetime, exported credentials, audience, session, audit correlation, cleanup, trust-policy changes, and incident revocation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Cloud Run release rebuilds after approval, sends all traffic before readiness, changes IAM, and cannot restore the prior digest. Deploy no-traffic, verify revision, canary signals, abort, rollback, and identity separation. Competency probe: Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option. A defensible solution applies Ci Cloud Run Revision Image Contract, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to progressive Cloud Run delivery runbook.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-cloud-run-revision-image-contract — Deploy one verified image digest with explicit service, region, configuration, runtime identity, revision suffix, labels, and no-traffic option.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-2
  - competency: ci-worked-cd-cloud-run-progressive-delivery-2
    artifact: cloud-run-delivery.yaml
    decision: worked cd cloud run progressive delivery 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      deploy-cloudrun-action: 3
      service: status-api
      region: us-central1
      image: registry.example.test/status@sha256:verified
      revision-suffix: run8842
      traffic: none-at-deploy
      smoke: authenticated-changed-case
      canary-percent: 5
      observe: latency-errors-user-success
      abort: explicit-threshold
      rollback: prior-revision-traffic
      iam: separately-owned
    verify: inspect revision image identity readiness authenticated smoke traffic totals observation window abort decision rollback and IAM audit
    failure: Reject latest tags, deploy acceptance as readiness, instant full traffic, representative-canary assumptions, rebuild rollback, or deployment-owned public IAM.
    repair: Deploy verified digest without traffic, smoke exact revision, canary with signals, abort predictably, move traffic back, and separate IAM.
content.caption: Runnable CI/CD workflow, artifact, release, and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.
